skill.name: postgresql-table-design
skill.description: Design PostgreSQL table schemas with practical guidance for data types, constraints, indexes, partitioning, JSONB, and schema evolution.
skill.summary: Guides PostgreSQL schema design decisions for durable, performant, and maintainable tables.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many dangerous patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown inline SQL examples and PostgreSQL terminology. No executable commands, scripts, network calls, secret access, malware behavior, or prompt injection attempts were found in SKILL.md. The skill is a reference guide for PostgreSQL table design and is safe to publish.

content.value_statement: PostgreSQL schema choices affect correctness, performance, and long-term maintenance. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical rules for table design, indexing, constraints, and migrations.
actual_capabilities: Recommends PostgreSQL data types for IDs, time, money, strings, JSONB, arrays, ranges, and vectors.
Explains primary keys, foreign keys, unique constraints, check constraints, and exclusion constraints.
Guides index selection for B-tree, GIN, GiST, BRIN, composite, partial, expression, and covering indexes.
Covers partitioning choices for large tables, time-series data, and maintenance workflows.
Provides guidance for update-heavy, insert-heavy, and upsert-friendly table designs.
Highlights safe schema evolution patterns for DDL, concurrent indexes, defaults, and dependency changes.
limitations: Does not inspect a live database or run query plans.
Does not generate migrations from an existing production schema automatically.
Requires workload details to choose the best indexes and partition keys.
Does not replace PostgreSQL documentation for version-specific behavior.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Product Schema
use_cases.description: Design normalized PostgreSQL tables with correct keys, constraints, timestamps, and indexes before implementation.

use_cases.title: Review Performance-Sensitive Tables
use_cases.description: Check whether indexes, partitioning, fillfactor, JSONB usage, and write patterns match expected queries.

use_cases.title: Improve Migration Safety
use_cases.description: Evaluate DDL plans for locking, rewrites, concurrent index creation, and dependency risks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Table Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Start a simple schema from requirements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design PostgreSQL tables for this feature. Include keys, required fields, data types, constraints, and essential indexes: [requirements].

prompt_templates.title: Schema Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Review an existing draft before implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this PostgreSQL schema for data type choices, constraints, indexes, and normalization issues: [schema description].

prompt_templates.title: Workload-Aware Indexing
prompt_templates.scenario: Tune indexes for known access patterns.
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these PostgreSQL tables and queries, recommend indexes and explain write cost tradeoffs: [tables and query patterns].

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Evolution Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan changes for a large production table.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a safe PostgreSQL schema evolution plan for this large table. Address locks, rewrites, backfills, and rollback steps: [change request].

output_examples.input: Design user and order tables for an ecommerce service.
output: A normalized table plan with users and orders as separate entities.
Recommended identity keys, required timestamps, status constraints, and money stored as exact numeric values.
Indexes for user lookups, order ownership, order creation time, and frequent status filters.

output_examples.input: Review a table that stores many optional profile fields in JSONB.
output: A recommendation to keep core relational fields as columns and reserve JSONB for variable attributes.
Suggested GIN indexing for containment searches and generated columns for heavily filtered scalar fields.
Warnings about query patterns that bypass indexes or make constraints harder to enforce.

output_examples.input: Plan a migration that adds fields to a large table.
output: A staged migration approach that avoids long blocking operations.
Guidance on defaults, nullable transitions, validation checks, and concurrent index creation.
Rollback considerations and verification steps for production deployment.

best_practices: Provide expected queries, write volume, and data growth when asking for schema guidance.
Start with normalized tables and denormalize only when measured query behavior justifies it.
Review PostgreSQL version support before using features such as UUID v7, NULLS NOT DISTINCT, or virtual generated columns.
anti_patterns: Do not add indexes for every column without matching query patterns.
Do not place core relational data in JSONB when constraints and joins are required.
Do not use timestamp without time zone for event time in multi-region or user-facing systems.

faq.question: Does this skill write migration files?
faq.answer: It can guide migration design, but it does not create framework-specific migration files by itself.

faq.question: Can it optimize a live PostgreSQL database?
faq.answer: It provides design guidance. Live tuning still needs query plans, metrics, and production workload data.

faq.question: Does it support JSONB schema design?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when JSONB is appropriate and how to index common JSONB access patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with time-series tables?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers partitioning, BRIN indexes, insert-heavy workloads, and TimescaleDB considerations.

faq.question: Is this skill PostgreSQL-specific?
faq.answer: Yes. Its guidance uses PostgreSQL behavior, data types, indexes, extensions, and schema evolution rules.

faq.question: Does it require database credentials?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guide and does not request or access credentials.
